verses.text: God tells Jeremiah to wear a linen girdle, then hide it by the river. The sign is acted out. God often taught His people through vivid, living parables.
God tells Jeremiah to wear a linen girdle, then hide it by the river. The sign is acted out. God often taught His people through vivid, living parables.
God tells Jeremiah to wear a linen girdle, then hide it by the river. The sign is acted out. God often taught His people through vivid, living parables.
God tells Jeremiah to wear a linen girdle, then hide it by the river. The sign is acted out. God often taught His people through vivid, living parables.
After many days the girdle is dug up, 'marred... profitable for nothing.' Buried away, it rotted. So pride and disobedience ruin what was meant to cling close and be useful.
After many days the girdle is dug up, 'marred... profitable for nothing.' Buried away, it rotted. So pride and disobedience ruin what was meant to cling close and be useful.
After many days the girdle is dug up, 'marred... profitable for nothing.' Buried away, it rotted. So pride and disobedience ruin what was meant to cling close and be useful.

As the girdle clings to the loins, so God 'caused to cleave unto me... the whole house of Israel,' for His praise; but they would not hear. They were meant to adorn Him. A people made for His glory marred themselves by refusing to obey.

As the girdle clings to the loins, so God 'caused to cleave unto me... the whole house of Israel,' for His praise; but they would not hear. They were meant to adorn Him. A people made for His glory marred themselves by refusing to obey.

Every wine bottle shall be filled; so God will fill the people with drunkenness and dash them one against another. Confusion is their judgment. The reeling of the drunkard pictures a people staggering to ruin.

Every wine bottle shall be filled; so God will fill the people with drunkenness and dash them one against another. Confusion is their judgment. The reeling of the drunkard pictures a people staggering to ruin.

'Give glory to the LORD your God, before he cause darkness'; if they will not hear, Jeremiah's soul will weep in secret for their pride. He pleads with tears. The faithful grieve over the obstinacy that calls down judgment.

'Give glory to the LORD your God, before he cause darkness'; if they will not hear, Jeremiah's soul will weep in secret for their pride. He pleads with tears. The faithful grieve over the obstinacy that calls down judgment.

'Give glory to the LORD your God, before he cause darkness'; if they will not hear, Jeremiah's soul will weep in secret for their pride. He pleads with tears. The faithful grieve over the obstinacy that calls down judgment.
Even king and queen are bidden, 'Humble yourselves,' for their crown shall fall and the cities be shut up. None is too high to be humbled. Pride on the throne is brought down with the rest.
Even king and queen are bidden, 'Humble yourselves,' for their crown shall fall and the cities be shut up. None is too high to be humbled. Pride on the throne is brought down with the rest.
When she asks why these things come, the answer is 'the greatness of thine iniquity.' Her shame is exposed. The calamity is no accident but the just fruit of great and long-cherished sin.

When she asks why these things come, the answer is 'the greatness of thine iniquity.' Her shame is exposed. The calamity is no accident but the just fruit of great and long-cherished sin.
'Can the Ethiopian change his skin, or the leopard his spots? then may ye also do good, that are accustomed to do evil.' Habitual sin enslaves. Only God can change a heart so set in its ways.
